title: Maximum Depth of Binary Tree
description:
Given a binary tree represented as `[val, left, right]`, return its **maximum depth**.

The maximum depth is the number of nodes along the longest path from the root down to the farthest leaf node.

Use `null` for missing children. Use `null` as the root for an empty tree.

**Example:**
```
root = [3, [9, null, null], [20, [15, null, null], [7, null, null]]]
Result = 3
```
hints: The depth of a tree is 1 + the max depth of its subtrees.
Base case: null node has depth 0.
This is a classic divide-and-conquer problem.
